summary: Decompiled and typed a substantial portion of Samus bomb item behavior, connected Samus down-B spawning code to a real item spawn helper, added Samus bomb attribute and item-var structs, and replaced several placeholder/UNK declarations with concrete prototypes. The PR author noted this was their first PPC decompilation work and that some register/stack matching details were uncertain, but the PR merged without visible review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented it_802B4AC8 as the Samus bomb spawn helper, constructing a SpawnItem for It_Kind_Samus_Bomb, copying the requested position, zeroing z and velocity, assigning parent gobj fields, setting facing_dir and spawn flags, calling Item_80268B18, and registering owner/debug linkage via it_802B4BA0 and db_80225DD8.
Reworked ftSs_SpecialLw_8012ADF0 to use Fighter_GObj/GET_FIGHTER, ftCheckThrowB0, a local Vec3 plus PAD_STACK(4), and the corrected it_802B4AC8 signature with only gobj, position, and facing_dir.
Added itSamusBombAttributes in itCharItems.h with seven f32 fields x0 through x18 and refined itSamusBomb_ItemVars from a padding/s32 placeholder into a bool x0 and Fighter_GObj* owner.
Implemented it_802B4BA0 to initialize Samus bomb velocity, item command variable, owner tracking, lifetime/attribute setup, collision/physics setup, and initial animation update.
Implemented itSamusbomb_UnkMotion0_Anim to apply a model/JObj update when lifeTimer falls below an attribute threshold, decrement the life timer, and transition to it_802B53CC when expired.
Implemented itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys with a local custom my_sqrtf helper using four frsqrte refinement iterations, then clamps XY velocity length to attr->xC via lbVector_NormalizeXY.
Implemented reflected behavior in it_2725_Logic50_Reflected, including it_80273030, upward bounce correction for negative y velocity using xC70, and owner clearing.

Implemented it_802B53CC as the transition into the explosion/motion-3 state, setting animation, frame speed, multiple item state/collision helpers, accessory callback it_802B5478, and a one-shot item-var flag.

Implemented it_802B5478 as an accessory callback that consumes the one-shot flag, checks the owner is still present, calls ftSs_Init_80128A1C, and notifies the Samus owner through ftSs_Init_80128944 using bomb position and hitbox scale.
Updated the Samus bomb event handler to clear samusbomb.owner instead of writing an untyped xDD8 integer.
Updated itsamusbomb.h and it_27CF.dox prototypes to remove an obsolete extra float parameter from it_802B4AC8 and replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for newly decompiled routines.
smart_moves: Used ftCheckThrowB0 instead of open-coding the throw_flags_b0 test/reset sequence, reducing local temporaries and matching the fighter-side idiom.
Preserved stack/register matching intent in ftSs_SpecialLw_8012ADF0 with PAD_STACK(4), explicitly addressing the author’s noted uncertainty around stack offsets.

Introduced a custom my_sqrtf with four reciprocal-square-root refinement iterations because this function did not match the common three-iteration pattern; the inline note documents a matching-specific exception future decompilers can reuse cautiously.

Converted Samus bomb item vars from anonymous padding/integer fields into semantic fields: a one-shot bool flag and a Fighter_GObj* owner, making later callback and reflection/event behavior easier to reason about.

Kept owner validity semantics explicit: spawn/init stores the owner, reflection and external event handling clear it, and the accessory callback only calls back into fighter code if owner remains non-NULL.
Synchronized function signatures across fighter call site, item header, implementation, and dox documentation after discovering it_802B4AC8 did not need the extra x parameter.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter article-spawn helper appears to take redundant position-offset data, verify whether the caller already applies the character attribute offset; in this PR Samus down-B adds x74_vec to the top joint position before calling the item helper, allowing it_802B4AC8 to drop the extra float argument.
Item xDD4 per-item vars should be typed as soon as behavior reveals meaning; owner pointers and one-shot flags produce clearer code than pad fields and generic integers.
Matching PPC math may require duplicating nonstandard refinement counts rather than relying on shared helpers; document local sqrt/frsqrte variants when iteration count differs.
Accessory callbacks can be used as delayed one-shot item-to-fighter notification points; model them with an explicit bool guard in item vars when behavior only fires once.
Header cleanup should accompany decompilation: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with real prototypes helps call sites and prevents stale parameter lists from surviving after implementation.
Use project macros and helpers such as GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, ftCheckThrowB0, VEC2_SQ_LEN, and lbVector_NormalizeXY to express known engine conventions and often improve matching.
naming_conventions: Samus fighter-special code keeps the ftSs_SpecialLw_ address-based naming convention, e.g. ftSs_SpecialLw_8012ADF0.

Samus bomb item functions remain under address-style it_802Bxxxx names where exact semantics are not fully named, while motion callbacks use the existing itSamusbomb_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll pattern.
New special-attribute struct is named itSamusBombAttributes and uses offset-style placeholder fields x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x14, x18 until field semantics are known.
New item-var struct fields use mixed semantic and offset-style names: bool x0 for an unknown one-shot flag and Fighter_GObj* owner for the identified owner pointer.
The item variable union path used by this PR is ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusbomb, matching the character item naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(4) in ftSs_SpecialLw_8012ADF0 to influence stack layout while keeping the decompiled logic concise.
Replaced manual bool temporary logic around throw_flags_b0 with ftCheckThrowB0, likely improving register allocation and matching while expressing the known side-effecting check.
Used a volatile float local in my_sqrtf and four __frsqrte Newton refinement steps to reproduce the target sqrt behavior for velocity clamping.
Kept vec.x offset multiplication inline as samus_attr->x74_vec.x * fp->facing_dir instead of carrying a separate vec_x temporary after the spawn helper signature was simplified.
Normalized XY velocity before scaling by the max-speed attribute, a common pattern for clamping vector length without changing direction.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/comment evidence says the author was unsure how to get registers and stack offset to match 100%, but believed the decompilation was correct.
Because the slice contains no reviewer discussion, there is no direct evidence of reviewer-requested naming, struct, or matching changes.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that rename itSamusBombAttributes fields x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14/x18 with semantic names.

Search for later fixes to the itsamusbomb.h it_802B4AC8 prototype, since the excerpt shows implementation/call-site using Fighter_GObj while the header line still displays Item_GObj in the first parameter.
Search for other PPC decomp PRs using custom sqrt helpers or nonstandard __frsqrte refinement counts.
Search for later Samus bomb work around itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Anim, which remained a placeholder in this excerpt.
Search for uses of ftSs_Init_80128A1C and ftSs_Init_80128944 to better identify the bomb-to-Samus callback semantics.
